content.full_text:
August 2, 1990

Ms. Jill Stecher, Staff Attorney  
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION  
428 "J" Street, Suite 800  
Sacramento, CA 95804-0807

RE: Alliance for Mission Viejo Charitable donation

Dear Ms. Stecher:

This is to confirm our recent telephone conversations regarding the above-referenced matter. In our first conversation I related the following facts to you:

The Alliance for Mission Viejo, a political action committee, accepted donations to conduct a recall campaign against a Mission Viejo city councilman. That recall election was held in February, 1990. There is a present balance in the Alliance's campaign fund in the sum of approximately $20,000.00. The steering committee of the Alliance has approved a cash contribution, pursuant to Gov't. Code Sec. 85803, to a local public school for the purchase of a large van. The Esperanza School in Mission Viejo is a special education school for the mentally retarded and it has a special need for a large vehicle to transport its students to jobs and other activities.

Based on these facts, you advised me, after looking into the matter, that this contribution could be properly made pursuant to Gov't. Code Sec. 85803.

JASPER & JASPER
ATTORNEYS AT LAW

Ms. Jill Stecher, Staff Attorney
August 2, 1990
Page 2

Thank you for your prompt attention to this matter. Because you acted so quickly, we will be able to get the van to the school before the beginning of the new term.

Very truly yours,

CATHERINE R. JASPER

CRJ:tmh
cc: Mr. Leonard Kraus, Chairman
     Ms. Colleen C. McAndrews
